sources.claim: OpenTelemetry represents standalone events as named occurrences at an instant in time with additional specifics, supporting structured classification rather than instruction-string ambiguity.
SLSA provenance describes how artifacts were produced so consumers can verify that production matched expectations and others can rebuild if needed.
Google SRE postmortem practice emphasizes action items that prevent recurrence, not just documenting historical failures.
OpenTelemetry migration guidance recognizes old and new semantic conventions can coexist, which supports quarantining historical evidence while enforcing new live guidance.

synthesis: Stage 8 should retain historical direct-call evidence as provenance while preventing those records from being interpreted as live boot instructions. The live path must be wrapper-only; historical callsites must be indexed and explicitly quarantined.
